text:
:

534 Brhat Samhitd
Dig a pit one cubit in diameter and twice as deep, fill it with milk
and water; and burn the pit when it becomes dry. Then smear it
(pit) with a mixture of honey, ghee and ashes. Then, first fill it with
mud to a height of four digits, then fill it with the flour of black
gram, sesamum and barley; again put mud over this to the same
height (4 digits). Again fill it with the flour of black gram etc.
Pour on it an infusion of fish and water and then pound all this
until it becomes a thick mass. Sow the seed at a depth of four digits
and sprinkle it with fish-water and flesh-water. Soon will a tendril
with excellent sprouts cover the bower in a marvellous manner.
[Utpala says that Syama, according to some, means Trivrt.
The author commits two grammatical errors in 23 ; For
he says and without a preposition is wrong. Here ^
means a beat in keeping time in music. The metre is except
in the last where it is i^cp^T.]
